observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to record the expense only upon receipt of the SSMI with duly validated original copy of the RIS, and maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records including the Supplies Ledger Cards for each commodity/stock;
b. Supply Officer Designate to submit SSMI along with the RIS to the Municipal Accountant and to maintain individual stock cards; and
c. Inventory Committee to conduct a semestral physical count of inventory items by type; prepare the RPCI and submit the results to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
Reiterated in Part II of this report as Observation No. 1.
observations.managementAction:
Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation. Inquiry revealed that the Municipal Accountant recorded expenses without receiving SSMI and RIS from the Supply Officer Designate.
Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation. No SSMI and RIS were submitted to the Municipal Accountant and stock cards were not maintained for CY 2024.
Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation. Physical count for inventory items was not conducted; thus, RPCIs were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management issue final demand letters to accountable persons who are not connected with the Municipality to immediately settle their accountabilities and consider taking legal actions against them.
Also, we recommended that the Management strictly comply with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2023-008 for the one-time cleansing of all dormant accounts of the LGU to prevent the balance of the account from accumulating further and to enhance the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The MSWDO and MAO committed to issue demand letters to the beneficiaries of loans with outstanding balances with coordination with the barangays.
The Management committed to adhere to the one-time cleansing of all dormant accounts of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management return the unexpended balances of fund transfers from NGAs of completed PPAs to the SAs or Bureau of Treasury.
Further, we recommended that the Management implement the PPAs as planned and monitor the utilization of funds from different NGAs by ensuring compliance with the MOAs in order to serve the intended purpose of the funds.
observations.managementAction:
Management returned some unexpended fund balances to SAs.
The Municipality was not able to return all the unexpended balances of fund transfers to SAs.
The Management committed to prioritize the implementation of these PPAs under TF Accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Finance Committee make representation through the Local Chief Executive to the Sangguniang Bayan for the revision and update of the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality pursuant to the provisions of Section 191 of RA No. 7160 in order to maximize its revenue raising ability for the delivery of basic services beneficial to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The executive and legislative departments committed to work closely for the passing of the revised revenue code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the requirements of localizing the Safe Spaces Act of 2019 to adopt measures localizing the applicability and ensure the enforcement of the law, in order to further promote the general welfare of the people.
observations.managementAction: There was already an initial review by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan (SP) on the draft local ordinance on localization of the Safe Spaces Act prepared by Sangguniang Bayan (SB) and was returned with review notes. After incorporating the noted clarification, it was forwarded back to the SP but until now Management has yet to receive the approved Ordinance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management facilitate the compliance of the exceptions noted by the legislative department allowing the immediate passage of the proposed ordinance of the lease of the Tourism Building.
We also recommended that the Management explore the possibility of the opening of the first level of the said building, pending the completion of the required improvement, in order to maximize the opportunity of generating revenue for the government.
observations.managementAction:
The management has set the Tourism Building to be operational this 2025 through enactment of the Tax Ordinance. Ordinance for the lease of the Tourism Building was not yet approved.
The Tourism Building is not yet open for lease.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the municipal officers concerned to adopt strategic measures to immediately carry out the programs/projects as planned in line with the appropriated amount in order to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and to deliver the timely expected benefits and needs due to the beneficiaries/constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has started in exerting its best efforts to accomplish the programmed PPAs and projects. The Management started to exert its best efforts to accomplish the programmed PPAs under the 20% DF composed of 14 projects in CY 2024. However, only four of these were implemented with a total cost of ₱4,755,864.88 or barely 19% of the total amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the concerned municipal officials to appropriate funds for the titling of the 36 parcel of land/lots and/or to register under the Torrens Title System as required under Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor has started the process on the titling of the parcel of lands owned by the LGU. As per Municipal Assessor, two lots were already registered under Torrens Title System; and the other lots are applied for titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the official/employee concerned must comply with the above requirements in order for him to be relieved from accountability.
observations.managementAction: The MAO had submitted documents to the former Audit Team for the request of relief from accountability of the cattle heifer which was reclassified to Other Receivables. Request was not granted. With regard to the issued properties that were missing, the Supply Officer prepared the list of missing and unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: Also, we recommended that the Management collect from the AOs the value of the properties that were lost and those which cannot be ascertained on its whereabouts.
observations.managementAction:
The Management committed to collect from AOs of the value of lost and unaccounted properties.
Management was not able to collect from the AOs the value of the lost properties.

observations.recommendation: Further, we recommended that the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) be renewed every three years or as often as necessary.
observations.managementAction:
Supply Officer committed to renew PAR of all issued properties.
The Supply Officer has started to update his records but PARs were not yet renewed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exert more effort in collecting from the beneficiaries their existing obligations.
observations.managementAction:
The concerned offices exerted effort in collecting the existing obligations of beneficiaries thru issuance of demand letters.
Reiterated in Part II of this report as Observation No. 6.

We recommended that the Management conduct regular review of fund and property accountabilities of accountable officers, including those officers who are authorized signatories and counter signatories in the issuance of checks and approval of DVs, to determine the appropriate amount of their fidelity bonds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall instruct the Accountant to: a. observe proper accounting treatment on accounts payable recognition and make the necessary adjustments to fairly present the affected liability, assets and expenses account of the year-end; and b. strictly comply with the timely withholding of taxes and effect the adjusting entries to recognize the withholding taxes portion of Accounts Payable.

We recommended that Management to require the Supply Officer Designate to update his records and prepare the Property transfer report and for the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the properties issued to public schools upon transfer.
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments on misclassified accounts to arrive at the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that Management require the: a. Municipal Accountant to record the expense only upon receipt of SSMI with duly validated original copy of the RIS, and maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records including the Supplies Ledger Cards; b. Supply Officer Designate to submit the RIS to the Municipal Accountant and to maintain individual stock cards; and c. Inventory Committee to conduct a semestral physical count of inventory items by type; prepare the RPCI and submit the results to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommended that Management issue final demand letters to accountable persons who are not connected with the Municipality to immediately settle their accountabilities and consider taking legal actions against them. Also, Management shall adhere to the one-time cleansing of all dormant accounts of the LGU to prevent the balance of the account from accumulating further and to enhance the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management return the unexpended balances from NGAs of these completed PPAs to the SAs or Bureau of Treasury. Further, we recommended that Management implement the PPAs as planned and monitor the utilization of funds from different NGAs by ensuring compliance with the MOAs in order to serve the intended purpose of the funds.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee make representation through the Local Chief Executive to the Sangguniang Bayan for the revision and update of the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality pursuant to the provisions of Section 191 of RA No. 7160 in order to maximize its revenue raising ability for the delivery of basic services beneficial to its constituents.

We recommended that Management conduct regular review of fund and property accountabilities of the Accountable Officers (AOs) including those officers who are signatories and counter signatories in the issuance of checks and approval of DVs, to determine the appropriate amount of their fidelity bonds. Also, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to apply for the increase in the fidelity bond of the AOs to commensurate their accountabilities to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended that Management comply with the requirements of localizing the Safe Spaces Act of 2019 to adopt measures localizing the applicability and ensure the enforcement of the law, in order to further promote the general welfare of the people.

We recommended Management will revise the scheme of payment with the therapy clinic by direct payment of the beneficiaries paying for the fee and secure the occupational therapy schedules that are convenient both for the clinic and the beneficiaries in order to achieve the full benefit of the program.

We recommended that the executive and legislative department shall work closely for the passing and approval of the Revenue Code and the Tourism Tax Ordinance. We also recommended to explore the possibility of the opening of the first level of the said building, pending the completion of the required improvement, in order to maximize the opportunity of generating revenue for the government.

We recommended Management will instruct the municipal officers concerned to adopt strategic measures to immediately carry out the implementation of the planned PPAs in line with the appropriated amount in order to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and to deliver the timely expected benefits to beneficiaries/constituents.

We recommended that Management instruct the Supply Officer to work on the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in order to maximize any realizable value from the disposal by sale and for the valuable spaces to be readily available for storages and other productive purposes.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned municipal officials to appropriate funds for the titling of the 36 parcel of land/lots complying for the requirements of titling of these lots under the Torrens Title System as required under Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that Management will require the official/employee concerned must comply with the above requirements in order for him to be relieved from accountability. The Municipal Accountant will record the accountability as receivable to the respective official/employee.
We recommended that Management make the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize the structures built over properties not owned by the municipality.

We recommended that the Accounting and the GSO-Designate together to reconcile the physical inventory count and the accounting record and verify the whereabouts of these unaccounted properties and conduct the preparation of the one-time cleansing of PPEs; collect from the accountable officers the value of the properties that were lost and those which cannot be ascertained on its whereabouts; and renew the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) every three years or as often as necessary.

We recommended that Management and the MSWDO and Agriculture Office exert more effort in collecting from the beneficiaries their existing obligations, identifying the existing beneficiaries their needed obligations and request for write-off to COA shall be made to those deceased beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation:
We hereby recommended that management shall undertake the following, viz:
- Control procedures on proper handling of cash collections and deposits
shall be carried out at all times.
- The Municipal Treasurer shall monitor monthly preparation of RAAF by
respective collectors and she shall use the same in the preparation of the
consolidated RAAF supported with documents of issued Accountable
Forms for the month, and so, discrepancies/error could be noted and
immediately corrected. She shall likewise monitor and control the issuance
of AF and make sure to keep all the documents to support the purchases
and issuances together with the complete copies of the duplicate and
triplicate copies of these issued ORs, deposit slip and the like, including
issued and unissued checks, in safe place for easy retrieval, and later on for
submission to COA and/or make it available to COA during cash
examination.
- The Municipal Treasurer shall provide the office of the accountant,
complete copy of official receipts and deposit slips together with the
monthly Report of Collections and Deposits, for the latter’s monitoring and
reconciliation and for recording in the agency’s books of accounts.
- The Municipal Accountant shall also make regular update of her books of
accounts specifically cash accounts Subsidiary Ledgers and tally balances
with the treasurer’s records, and so required certification needed to
support Report of Cash Examination could be easily provided to the office
of the auditor.
- Accountable forms shall be issued chronologically, and ensure that monthly
report of accountability for accountable forms tallies with the actual
accountable forms on hand.
- Collections shall be remitted and deposited daily or if collections reach the
amount of P5,000.00, to avoid accumulation of the same in the hands of the
collecting officers and Municipal Treasurers, and avoid possible loss or
misappropriation thereof.
- The Municipal Treasurer and collectors shall declare all collections with
issued ORs and present the same to COA representative at the time of cash
count. Failure to do so would be taken negatively, shortages would be
investigated and reported, and corresponding penalty will be properly
imposed.

We hereby recommended the following:
- Existing practice of the agency shall be discontinued as this is not the proper
way of processing claims and it may further create adverse impact to agency
and to concerned officials if this will continue to exist in their transactions.
- Submit a written justification explaining therein the reasons for processing
claims without complete and appropriate documentation.
- The Municipal Accountant shall likewise submit all the required
documentation needed to establish the validity and legality of the
disbursements and henceforth, payment shall only be made for claims
supported with complete documentation.

We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Treasurer shall render her monthly reports such as
Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) with supporting documents, Payrolls,
Official Receipts and other liquidation documents including revenue reports
and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th
day of the ensuing month to allow its prompt recording in the books of
accounts.
- The Municipal Accountant shall coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to
ensure prompt submission of DVs and other related reports, undertake
recording in the books of accounts, and submit the same within 10 days of
the ensuing month to the Office of the Auditor.
- Concerned officials shall prioritize and adopt measures to address issues on
delayed submission of reports to be able to meet the deadline set and be free
from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof as required
under article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
- Require concerned official/s to submit written justification explaining
therein the reasons for late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate
actions shall be instituted against the accountable officers for the
40
delayed/non submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of
the Revised Penal Code.

We hereby recommended to management the following:
- To faithfully comply and enforce the periodic submission of these reports
within the prescribed period. Concerned officials shall likewise prioritize
submission of these reports and look into the root cause of these delays and
immediately initiate measures to address the problem.
- Require concern official/s to submit written justification explaining therein
the reasons of late submission, and if not satisfied, appropriate actions shall
be instituted against the accountable officers for the delayed/non
submission of financial reports as required under Article 218 of the Revised
Penal Code.

Take heed to the required guidelines on proper documentation as well as on the
prescribed timelines on reports submission, especially those transactions
involving credit financing as usage of the loan and payment of amortization are
strictly monitored, since the amount is substantial that improper
documentation thereof would expose government interest to possible risk and
may result to further loss of government funds.

We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer shall exert
more efforts to maintain their respective accounting and budget records,
using the prescribed form, and update the same regularly.
- The necessity of additional workforce, who has the required expertise and
qualification, for the two (2) offices shall be properly considered and shall
be prioritized as well, including their attendance to trainings/seminars to
keep them abreast of the latest accounting and budget issuances.

We hereby recommended the following:
- Intensify efforts for the timely conduct of annual physical count of PPE and
submit the required Reports on PPE to COA within deadline set.
- Invite representative from COA to witness the said inventory taking.
Invitation letter shall be sent three (3) days prior to the scheduled date to
give ample time to prepare and settle whatever time inconveniences that may
have.
- Prioritize the update of the property and accounting records including PPE
subsidiary ledger, and immediately present the same to the office of the
auditor for verification and review.

We recommended to management the following:
- Determine the whereabouts of the checks and provide us of its status
immediately thereafter.
- The Municipal Treasurer through the Municipal Accountant shall submit to
COA all checks that has been reported as cancelled.
- Ensure that all Report of Checks Issued shall bear copy of cancelled checks
(if there is any) to comply with the above-mentioned regulation. However, if
these checks could no longer be found despite diligent search and effort, this
has to be immediately reported to Head of Agency and to COA, and likewise,
submit the aforementioned required reports to COA immediately thereafter.

We recommended to management the following:
- Submit all other transaction documents and so we could complete our audit
on GAD Funds and refrain from processing GAD transaction not duly
supported with documents.
- Immediately submit all other requirements in settlement of audit suspension
to avoid disallowance in audit; and it will facilitate completion of the audit on
GAD fund as well.

Immediately submit all other documents to settle audit suspension to avoid
disallowance in audit; and it will facilitate completion of the audit on LDRRM
fund as well.

We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so
only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required project validation
and ocular inspection could be promptly undertaken as well.

1.5 We recommended that Management replace the current OSCA Head and
comply with the requirements of RA No. 9994 in the appointment of OSCA
Head to give chance to other qualified senior citizens to hold the office and to
promote innovations within the organization.

2.4 We recommended that the Management review the existing organizational
structure and consider terminating the contracts of employees that duplicate
existing work in order to reduce labor cost.

3.6 We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the requirements
of localizing the Safe Spaces Act of 2019 to adopt measures localizing the
applicability and ensure the enforcement of the law, in order to further
promote the general welfare of the people.

4.5 We recommended that the LGU employees refund the excess CNA Incentive
received due to insufficiency of funds to finance the said incentive.

5.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should require the claims
for hazard pay be supported by an established procedure identifying the
actual personnel that are actually exposed to risks as identified in the above
cited DBM and DSWD Joint Circular and with a report of actual service prior
to payment of Hazard Pay.

6.5 We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to collect the fees embodied
in the Municipal Revenue Code.
b. Deputize barangay collector, if necessary, to augment manpower
requirement in collecting fees in various barangays.
c. Establish guideline in determining the additional fees to be collected
from those located outside the town proper.

7.5 We recommended that Management revise the scheme of payment with the
therapy clinic by direct payment instead of the beneficiaries paying for the fee
and secure therapy session schedules that are convenient both for the clinic
and the beneficiaries in order to achieve the full benefit of the program.

8.5 We recommended that Management facilitate compliance of the exceptions
noted by the legislative department allowing the immediate passage of the
proposed ordinance of the lease of the Tourism Building. We also
recommended to explore the possibility of the opening of the first level of the
said building, pending the completion of the required improvement, in order
to maximize the opportunity of generating revenue for the government.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- The Municipal Accountant shall not process claims/payments not duly
supported with required documents. She shall likewise follow correct
disbursement system at all times.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in
other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

Recommendation:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Municipal Accountant shall prioritize submission of the COVID related
reports and transactions documents since the prescribed period within which
to submit has already lapsed, and the necessity of its immediate audit has
been laid down to establish proper utilization thereof.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The Accounting, Supply and Property Units shall update regularly, PPE
Subsidiary Ledgers, Stock Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and
reconcile balances of these records with the Report on Physical Count of
Inventories and PPE to be able to reflect a complete and accurate balances in
the financial statements.
- The management shall likewise include in their yearly plan the conduct of the
required physical count of Inventories and PPE to be undertaken by the
agency’s Inventory Committee, and so required reports can be prepared and
submitted to COA on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.
- Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness
in every inventory-taking to comply with the regulation. Invitation letter
shall be served and stamped received by COA at least three days prior to the
scheduled date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle whatever time
inconveniences that may have.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall faithfully follow correct disbursement system and so
only valid and proper claims will be processed and paid.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit, and the required ocular inspection
could be promptly undertaken as well.
- Be guided of the pertinent sections in the afore-mentioned regulations and in
other GPPB issued resolutions simplifying the requirements in project
implementation during emergency cases.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall faithfully adhere to the
provisions stated in the afore-mentioned regulations in relation to the
deduction, withholding and remittance of required taxes specifically on goods and
services, and remit the same within the required timeframe which is every 10th of the
ensuing month.

Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- Exert all possible efforts to collate all the lacking documents and/or prepare a
written justification for the non-settlement of the audit suspensions, and
submit the same to COA for the necessary evaluation and review, otherwise,
the transactions covered by it will be disallowed in audit.
- Refrain from undertaking a project not supported with complete bid and
technical documentation. However, awarding of contract shall only be made to
a legally, financially and technically capable supplier/contractor and
willing to cooperate in the submission of required documents.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the preparation of
documents and settlement of all audit suspensions to be able to meet the
required deadlines.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
- Prioritize submission of the monthly report of transactions to COA within
the deadline set to avoid negative repercussion; and timely audit of its
transactions could be undertaken as well.
- Maximize work assignment among accounting personnel and/or assign
additional staff, if the present workforce is not enough, so as to prevent work
overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended to management the following:
- The Municipal Accountant shall ensure that the required supporting
documents of all claims are complete before affixing her/his signature in
Box A of the Disbursement Voucher.
- The management shall likewise observe that proper flow of transactions is
strictly followed so that only valid and proper claims are processed and
paid.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall stamp “PAID” all DVs
and its supporting documents after payment was made, to prevent the re-use of
the same on similar transaction.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following:
- The management shall verify status of its approval with the National Solid
Waste Management Commission, if already submitted to NSWMC, and so
deficiencies could be noted, and submission of the lacking documents could
be immediately made.
- Complete other required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management
and immediately submit copy of the same to COA including the creation of
the Solid Waste Management Board and proof in the establishment of the
required solid waste facilities.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended to management the following:
- The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the agency’s
Technical Working Group (TWG) shall adhere to R.A 9184 and to other
procurement rules and regulations in all their procurement undertakings.
- Submit all lacking documentation to enable us complete our audit on the said
fund and further undertake the required ocular inspection. Moreover,
refrain from processing claims/payments not duly supported with complete
documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
- Observe deadlines in reports submission for a timely audit and immediate
inclusion of its results in the Annual Audit report.

Recommendation:
We recommended to management that projects to be implemented out of the
Local Development Fund shall be within the authorized projects stated in the
requirement set-forth in the afore-mentioned regulation.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management the following:
- The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the agency’s
Technical Working Group (TWG) shall adhere to R.A 9184 and to other
procurement rules and regulations in all their procurement undertakings.
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall discontinue
processing of claims/payments for LDRRM related projects and activities
without appropriate documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and
disallowance.
- Concerned officials shall submit all lacking documentation including un-
submitted Disbursement Vouchers to enable us complete our audit on the
said fund.

Recommendations:
- Fast track finalization of the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan and
have it approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to
ensure that programs, projects and activities included in the plan are in
accordance with the National Solid Waste Management Framework.
- Complete other required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management
and submit copy to COA immediately thereafter.
- Ensure that proper mechanisms, like budget and manpower, are in place to
give assurance to proper implementation of the plan.
- Solid waste management programs/projects must align with the approved
SWM Plan. Any deviation from the said plan could be a ground for
disallowance in audit.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other
regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.

Recommendations:
We recommended to management the following:
- Determine the whereabouts of these checks and provide us of its status
immediately thereafter.
- The Municipal Treasurer through the Municipal Accountant shall submit to
COA all checks that have been reported as cancelled.
- Extra effort shall be exerted in locating the missing checks. However, if these
checks could no longer be found despite diligent search and effort, this has to
be immediately reported to Head of Agency and to COA, and likewise apply
for a relief of accountability and submit justification letter for late filing of
relief of accountability to COA together with other above-mentioned
documents.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended the following measures:
- The Municipal Treasurer shall coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in the
preparation of the required Certified List of Taxpayers and immediately
provide copy of the same to the Municipal Accountant to be able to establish
accurate amount of basic RPT and SET receivables.
- Upon receipt of the latest Certified List of Taxpayers, the Municipal
Accountant shall make necessary adjusting entries to correct the recorded
amount of RPT/SET Receivable vis a vis the submitted certified list of
39
taxpayers.
- The accounting office shall likewise maintain an index card for each taxpayer
to facilitate the establishment of the accurate amount of collectibles from
each taxpayer.
- Regularly update RPT/SET Receivable accounts Subsidiary Ledgers.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
- The management shall always formulate GAD Plan with great emphasis
on proper planning and compliance to various GAD policies and
regulations.
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall discontinue
processing of claims/payments for GAD related projects and activities
without appropriate documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and
disallowance.
- Concerned officials shall submit to COA all lacking documentation
including un-submitted Disbursement Vouchers to enable us complete our
audit on the said fund.

Recommendation:
We recommended to management to enforce collection strategies and remedies
to increase RPT/SET collections as well as undertake measures to require all
delinquent taxpayers to prioritize payment of tax on Real Property within the
deadlines and in accordance with existing regulations.

Recommendation:
We hereby recommended to concerned officials to exert all efforts to locate
these lacking Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and immediately submit the same
to COA together with other required documentation. However, if DVs were not
prepared before check issuance, we further recommended in discontinuing the
practice of issuing check without the duly approved Disbursement Voucher
supported with proper and complete documentation. A sound internal control
system shall be put in place and carried out at all times.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
- Discontinue payment of claims not supported with complete
documentations.
- Observe correct disbursement system.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the settlement of all
audit suspensions to be able to meet the required deadlines.

Recommendations:
We hereby recommended to management the following:
- Head of Agency shall request and/or demand in writing from the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions,
and submit the same together with other supporting documents to the
Accounting Section within the time prescribed by regulation.
- Head of the accounting section shall prioritize submission of transaction
documents and other financial reports to COA and maximize work
assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload
and meet deadlines on reports submission.
- Take heed to the prescribed timeframe on submission of accounts and
financial reports to avoid sanctions that may be imposed from violating
thereof as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides that:
Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the president,
any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with
any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty
and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against
the said public officer who, with the degree of his guilt in accordance
with the civil service law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with
the requirements impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof the
public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.

Recommendation:
We hereby recommended that concerned official shall maximize budgeted
amount for individual plans and programs to be able to meet its minimum
requirement, and likewise, submit immediately all the required documentation
relative to its proper implementation together with the breakdown of its actual
expenditures and other necessary data to facilitate the conduct of our post-audit
activity.

Recommendation:
We recommend to management to adhere with the aforementioned regulation
on the releases of barangay shares, which is within five (5) days after the end of
each quarter.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to be compliant to provision
stated in RMO No. 23-2012 in the withholding and remittance of taxes on goods
and services. Moreover, submit immediately all other documents specifically
those involving tax remittances to enable us confirm and validate compliance to
afore-cited regulations.

Recommendations:
The management shall undertake the following:
- Comply with the required laws, rules and regulations on LDRRM to avoid
sanction that may be imposed for violating thereof.
- The Municipal Budget Officer shall prepare and maintain separate LDRRM
Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for QRF, MOOE
and Capital Outlay as required in the afore-mentioned regulation.
- The Municipal Accountant shall also prepare and maintain subsidiary
records for LDRRM and make it available to COA for the necessary review
and verification; and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements.

Recommendation:
Municipal Budget Officer shall always takes heed to the requirement of the law
and ensure that budget records were maintained and updated regularly to be
able to provide reliable information towards fair presentation of agency’s
Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to –
immediately coordinate with the Supply Officer to reconcile the PPE balances between their records and effect the necessary adjusting entries, where appropriate, to reflect the correct PPE year-end balances for fair presentation in the Financial Statement, and
see to it that reconciliation between the records of the Accounting and Supply Officer should be performed regularly pursuant to Section C.3 Chapter V of the Manual on Property of Custodianship and Section 114 of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of account/GL the unrecorded subject properties and to reflect the correct year-end balances of the affected PPE accounts for fair presentation in the Financial Statement. Likewise, see to it that the PPEs held for distribution and donation are included in the RCPPE pursuant to Section 114 of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provisions under Item 4.1.6 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in order to avoid misuse, mismanagement or unaccounted loss of the Petty Cash Fund and to properly charged to the accountable person,  in case of loss.
We recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 93 of PD 1445 to prevent payment of fictitious claims and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management instruct the Human Resource Officer to require every employee to submit monthly individual DTR to support thereof the payment of salaries and wages. Further, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that Disbursement Vouchers for payment of payroll should be supported with the required DTR as proof of the services being rendered.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the municipal personnel/drivers concerned to submit properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets pursuant to Specific Rules and Regulations No. 2 and No. 7 of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 to avoid disallowance in audit.

Likewise, if in case the odometer is out of order, we recommended that Management have the odometer be repaired or replaced at once. The driver of motor vehicle concerned shall be held responsible for failure to report defective odometer as well as the responsible official who neglects to undertake the repair thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Officer concerned to immediately prepare and furnish a copy to the Audit Team the required MOA in relation to the distribution/release of the agricultural equipment to the different barangays to avoid possible risk on the misuse/improper use thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to recognize in the book of accounts and in the RCPPE the cellular phone units availed under a postpaid plan subscription and issue a corresponding PAR to the accountable officer concerned.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the subject Disbursement Vouchers together with the other related supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for post audit and to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension in audit. Likewise, strictly observe and comply with the prescribed timeline in the submission of accounts to the Auditor as provided in Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the municipal officials concerned to immediately distribute the subject agricultural equipment to the intended beneficiaries in order to achieve optimum and timely benefits derived therefrom and increase their productive capabilities. In the meantime that such were not yet distributed, keep these properties in a secured area to avoid theft and further deterioration thereof.

We recommended that Management be more prudent in the development of all its projects, programs and activities and properly plan the timing of procurement and implementation of the same in order to optimize the utilization of said projects, programs and activities and derived the full benefits therefrom.

We recommended that management instruct the municipal officers concerned to adopt strategic measures to immediately carry out the programs/projects as planned in line with the appropriated amount in order to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and to deliver the timely expected benefits and needs due to the beneficiaries/constituents.

Make necessary journal entries to record all parcels of land owned by the municipality under Land Account – General Fund following the prescribed valuation in PPSAS 17 to be able to correct understatement of PPE account. Identify those lots recorded in the books without the required legal documents of ownership as those should be properly disclosed in the notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Reiterated findings. Still in the process of gathering evidence of rightful ownership. Concerned officials shall continue to work on the titling of the aforementioned lots to be able to provide proof of ownership and us the interest of the municipality on the properties is secured, and likewise, ensures the legitimacy of recording in the books. Provide us details of the land account recorded in the books including copy of its updated Subsidiary Ledger (SL).
observations.reason: Lot owned by the agency yet to be recorded in the agency’s books of accounts. Still in the process of gathering evidence of rightful ownership.

observations.recommendation: Formulate Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan and have it approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission. Complete required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management and submit copy to COA. Ensure mechanisms like budget and manpower are in place. Solid waste management programs/projects must align with the approved SWM Plan and fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Part II Reiterated findings. The management has a draft copy of the Solid Waste Management Plan and is working on revisions to complete the plan. They are exerting efforts to come up with an effective SWMP and to secure approval by the NSWMC. Complete with required documentation/data and submit copy to COA. Fast track finalization.
observations.reason: Lack of technical know-how among concerned officials, preparation of the said plan still on going and beyond the target date of completion.

observations.recommendation: Instruction shall be given to concerned officials to complete the required documentation and collating all the lacking documents and submit the same to COA immediately thereafter and to COA inspectorate for necessary inspection and validation. Discontinue processing of claims/payments for Local Development Projects without the pre-implementation requirements and other required documentations so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Reiterated findings. The management instructed concerned officials to complete preparation, collation and submission of the lacking documents and to settle all related audit suspensions; otherwise, notices of disallowance may be issued. They will discontinue processing of claims/payments without the required documents.

Recommendation:
As the grant of financial assistance to indigent families has always been one of the municipality’s major programs, we have recommended that the grant be documented as follows:
Maintain a client logbook
Interview of client and submission of requirement to establish eligibility
Request for Financial Assistance duly approved by Mun. Mayor
Certificate of Eligibility as Indigent signed by MSWDO
Certificated of Death from Hospital or Barangay Captain for Burial Assistance
Medical Certificate from Hospital for Medical Assistance
Report from Bureau of Fire Protections or any authorized office for Fire Assistance
Schools statement of accounts for Educational Assistance
Acknowledgement Receipt
Likewise, a set of predetermine amount for each type of financial assistance should be established to institutionalize a non- discretionary determination of amount given to each client.

Recommendation:
The management shall review and adopt systematic procedures in the processing of claims/disbursements that will provide a check and balance of functions and accountabilities.
Direct all non performing officials to handle their assigned task with diligent and care as well as consider assigning additional personnel to accounting unit and to other key offices whose reports are significant in decision making, and so the reports can be prepared promptly and be able to meet deadlines on report submission, and be unconstrained from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof  as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides that:
Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the president, any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against the said public officer who, with the degree of his guilt in accordance with the civil service law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof the public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.
Discontinue the practice of processing claims/payments without proper and complete documentation.
Strictly observe the correct disbursement system.

We hereby recommended the following:
Concerned officials shall give utmost importance to proper procurement planning where actual needs, right quantity and right processes, among others are considered.  Further, procurement above the threshold shall be done thru procurement service or public bidding to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the municipality

Stop the practice of splitting requisition and purchase order. Items needed for one quarter and/or single activity/project shall be consolidated and procured in one purchase order.

Include in CY 2017 action plan to be registered with PhilGeps to be able to fully utilize the PhilGeps and meets its objective on promoting transparency and efficiency in   procurement process and to give all interested parties an equal opportunity to bid.
